data.title: As a project onboarder, my existing merge.ci_workflow_name is auto-migrated to ci.workflow_name
data.description:
## Description

**What**: When onboarding detects merge.ci_workflow_name in an existing dso-config.conf, automatically write the value to ci.workflow_name and log a deprecation notice. When ci.workflow_name already exists, skip migration and log that the old key can be manually removed.
**Why**: merge.ci_workflow_name was deprecated in favor of ci.workflow_name but no migration path exists in onboarding — users with existing configs silently keep the deprecated key.
**Scope**:
- IN: Auto-migration logic in SKILL.md Phase 3 config generation; deprecation notice logging; conditional skip when ci.workflow_name already present
- OUT: Removing merge.ci_workflow_name from KNOWN_KEYS (still valid for backwards compat); changes to merge-to-main.sh behavior

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running onboarding on a project with merge.ci_workflow_name in existing dso-config.conf auto-migrates the value to ci.workflow_name without blocking for user input
  ← Satisfies: "SC3 — deprecation auto-migration"
- When this story is complete, running onboarding on a project where both merge.ci_workflow_name and ci.workflow_name exist skips migration and logs that the old key can be removed
  ← Satisfies: "SC3 — both-keys-coexist handling"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must handle edge case where both keys exist — conditional on ci.workflow_name absence
- [Maintainability] All SKILL.md stories modify the same file — sequential execution recommended
